Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

I guess I don't see how the fundamental relationships are changed - The .fscrypt directory is always global, since it's at the root.  This just makes it possible for the subdir mount to find it.

The relationship between encryption of different directories seems unchanged to me?

Patch Set 2:

> The relationship between encryption of different directories seems
 > unchanged to me?

Exactly. And having a single .fscrypt directory (at the root of the fs) gives a consistent view to fscrypt.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

This is a nice solution. I wonder if we could use something similar for .lustre/lost+found. Currently lfsck doesn't work with filesets.

Patch Set 2:

Currently open by fid does not work with filesets either. That's another good candidate.

Patch Set 2:

"Currently open by fid does not work with filesets either"

Why not?  Specifically open-by-fid and not, like, fid2path?

Patch Set 2:

> "Currently open by fid does not work with filesets either"
 > 
 > Why not?  Specifically open-by-fid and not, like, fid2path?

.lustre/fid is not made available with filesets, so for me open-by-fid is not possible. But I checked and fid2path works with filesets.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

> This is a nice solution. I wonder if we could use something similar
 > for .lustre/lost+found. Currently lfsck doesn't work with filesets.

Not sure what you mean?  LFSCK itself runs on the server, so it will always work.  Access to lost+found is restricted, and should remain so, because there is no way to know which files in lost+found belong to which subdirectory mount, and making them globally available may be a security leak. 

Similarly for .lustre/fid - allowing this to containers may allow access to files outside the container, which may be a security leak.

parents.subject: LU-15452 utils: support lctl getattr for osc

commit.subject: LU-15176 sec: present .fscrypt in subdir mount
commit.message:
LU-15176 sec: present .fscrypt in subdir mount

fscrypt userspace tool works with a .fscrypt directory at the root of
the file system. In case of subdirectory mount, we virtually present
this .fscrypt directory at the root of the mount point so that fscrypt
can be used. This makes it possible to even do a subdirectory mount of
an encrypted directory, making clients access encrypted content only.
Internally, the .fscrypt directory is always stored at the root of
Lustre.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I2a0ee360f724da1df49b2be0df986d52e06f45fd
